economic_notes: First Nation Business Trust owns Cree Land Mini-Mart Limited Partnership and North Piapot Holdings Limited Partnership. First Nation has 8.3% interest in Keseechiwan Holdings Inc. (Swift Current Casino Development). Non-controlling interest in 2544737 Ontario Limited (soft drink and ice manufacturing). COVID-19 impacts included office closures, travel restrictions, and increased costs. Multiple claims initiated against Government of Canada for damages.
notes: Fiscal year 2022 (ended March 31, 2022). Province is Saskatchewan (Treaty 4 Territory). The auditor issued an Emphasis of Matter paragraph noting multiple claims initiated against the Government of Canada for damages, with loans taken to fund negotiations and insurance to cover repayment if claims are insufficient. Net debt position of $17.57M. Large increase in deferred revenue from $2.83M to $14.33M mainly due to Sewage Pumping Station/Lagoon Expansion ($6.47M) and Rapid Housing Initiative ($2.89M). The modified equity method entity (Piapot First Nation Business Trust) has a December 31 year-end, creating a timing difference. Three RBC term loans ($2.2M, $570K, $293K) related to litigation claims excluded from 5-year principal repayment schedule. CMHC replacement reserve underfunded. total_government_transfers estimated as ISC ($14,970,383) + CMHC Subsidy ($755,872) + CMHC Rent ($244,080) + CMHC RRAP ($35,600) + FHQ Tribal Council ($686,589) - note FHQ may not be government; if excluded total is $16,005,935. OSR calculated as total revenue minus government transfers.
